### Post by gareththomasnz on 2009-07-02
I know many people have this problem and I just spent about 5 hours trying to alter my refresh rate to no avail. Just going around in circles.

My Monitor philips 107T

according to nvidia-settings my video device is GeForce 8400 GS 

my xorg.conf is as follows:

# xorg.conf (X.Org X Window System server configuration file)
#
# This file was generated by dexconf, the Debian X Configuration tool, using
# values from the debconf database.
#
# Edit this file with caution, and see the xorg.conf manual page.
# (Type "man xorg.conf" at the shell prompt.)
#
# This file is automatically updated on xserver-xorg package upgrades *only*
# if it has not been modified since the last upgrade of the xserver-xorg
# package.
#
# If you have edited this file but would like it to be automatically updated
# again, run the following command:
#   sudo dpkg-reconfigure -phigh xserver-xorg

Section "InputDevice"
    Identifier    "Generic Keyboard"
    Driver        "kbd"
    Option        "XkbRules"    "xorg"
    Option        "XkbModel"    "pc105"
    Option        "XkbLayout"    "us"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
    Identifier    "Configured Mouse"
    Driver        "mouse"
    Option        "CorePointer"
EndSection

Section "Device"
    Identifier    "Configured Video Device"
    Driver        "nvidia"
    Option        "NoLogo"    "True"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
    Identifier    "Configured Monitor"
        HorizSync   30-71
        VertRefresh 50-160
 # Modeline "1280x1024"   185.64   1280 1376 1600 2024   1024 1024 1028 1079 +hsync +vsync

EndSection

Section "Screen"
    Identifier    "Default Screen"
    Monitor        "Configured Monitor"
    Device        "Configured Video Device"
    Defaultdepth    24
EndSection

Section "ServerLayout"
    Identifier    "Default Layout"
  screen "Default Screen"
EndSection
Section "Module"
    Load        "glx"
EndSection



The         HorizSync   30-71
        VertRefresh 50-160

are correct for my monitor - the modeline I added didn't seem to change a darn thing as all I can get is 50 hertz at 1280 x 1024

I would like to have 85 hertz at whatever resolution - the present screen size is OK but the refresh rate sucks.


I tried all sorts of stuff - cant seem to get it to work

nvidia driver 169.12


Please help if you know what you are doing - cheers

---

### Post by CatKiller on 2009-07-02
> **gareththomasnz said:**
> all I can get is 50 hertz at 1280 x 1024

If you check NVidia's own resolution tool, it will report the correct refresh rate. It won't be 50Hz. Their driver deliberately mis-reports the refresh rate to xrandr. If you want to change that behaviour, put ```
        Option          "DynamicTwinView"               "False"
``` in the Device section.

---

### Post by gareththomasnz on 2009-07-03
The nvidia tool says its 60.02 Hz

Still not what I want - I want 85 Hz

I ran cvt 1280 1024 refresh 85 and got the correct modeline

# 1280x1024 84.84 Hz (CVT 1.31M4) hsync: 91.46 kHz; pclk: 159.50 MHz
Modeline "1280x1024_85.00"  159.50  1280 1376 1512 1744  1024 1027 1034 1078 -hsync +vsync

Heres my xorg.conf now:

# xorg.conf (X.Org X Window System server configuration file)
#
# This file was generated by dexconf, the Debian X Configuration tool, using
# values from the debconf database.
#
# Edit this file with caution, and see the xorg.conf manual page.

# This file is automatically updated on xserver-xorg package upgrades *only*
# if it has not been modified since the last upgrade of the xserver-xorg
# package.
#
# If you have edited this file but would like it to be automatically updated

Section "InputDevice"
    Identifier    "Generic Keyboard"
    Driver        "kbd"
    Option        "XkbRules"    "xorg"
    Option        "XkbModel"    "pc105"
    Option        "XkbLayout"    "us"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
    Identifier    "Configured Mouse"
    Driver        "mouse"
    Option        "CorePointer"
EndSection

Section "Device"
    Identifier    "Configured Video Device"
    Driver        "nvidia"
    Option        "NoLogo"    "True"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
    Identifier    "Configured Monitor"
        HorizSync   30-71
        VertRefresh 50-160
Modeline "1280x1024_85.00"  159.50  1280 1376 1512 1744  1024 1027 1034 1078 -hsync +vsync
EndSection

Section "Screen"
    Identifier    "Default Screen"
    Monitor        "Configured Monitor"
    Device        "Configured Video Device"
    SubSection "Display"
        Modes        "1280x1024_85.00"
    EndSubSection
    Defaultdepth    24
EndSection

Section "ServerLayout"
    Identifier    "Default Layout"
  screen "Default Screen"
EndSection
Section "Module"
    Load        "glx"
EndSection

So what am I doing wrong ? Am I supposed to put some mode info in the "screen" section ?

---

### Post by CatKiller on 2009-07-03
> **gareththomasnz said:**
> My Monitor philips 107T

Everything I've found on this monitor says that it doesn't do 1280×1024@85. If you want an 85Hz refresh rate you'll need to drop it down to 1024×768. You can get 75Hz at 1152×864 according to the manual. But the highest resolution can only be done at a refresh rate of 60Hz.

---

### Post by gareththomasnz on 2009-07-03
Thankyou --> OK you are correct - my manual didn't have all the info so I downloaded another.

I was running it at 85 Hz in windows - which may have been harmful

So now it is at 75 Hz which is tolerable but I miss 85 Hz.

Here is the xorg.conf that works:

# xorg.conf (X.Org X Window System server configuration file)
#
# This file was generated by dexconf, the Debian X Configuration tool, using
# values from the debconf database.
#
# Edit this file with caution, and see the xorg.conf manual page.
# (Type "man xorg.conf" at the shell prompt.)
#
# This file is automatically updated on xserver-xorg package upgrades *only*
# if it has not been modified since the last upgrade of the xserver-xorg
# package.
#
# If you have edited this file but would like it to be automatically updated
# again, run the following command:
#   sudo dpkg-reconfigure -phigh xserver-xorg

Section "InputDevice"
    Identifier    "Generic Keyboard"
    Driver        "kbd"
    Option        "XkbRules"    "xorg"
    Option        "XkbModel"    "pc105"
    Option        "XkbLayout"    "us"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
    Identifier    "Configured Mouse"
    Driver        "mouse"
    Option        "CorePointer"
EndSection

Section "Device"
    Identifier    "Configured Video Device"
    Driver        "nvidia"
    Option        "NoLogo"    "True"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
    Identifier    "Configured Monitor"
        HorizSync   30-71
        VertRefresh 50-160
Modeline "1152x864_75.00"  104.00  1152 1224 1344 1536  864 867 871 905 -hsync +vsync
#Modeline "1024x768_85.00"   94.50  1024 1096 1200 1376  768 771 775 809 -hsync +vsync
#Modeline "1280x1024_85.00"  159.50  1280 1376 1512 1744  1024 1027 1034 1078 -hsync +vsync
EndSection

Section "Screen"
    Identifier    "Default Screen"
    Monitor        "Configured Monitor"
    Device        "Configured Video Device"
    SubSection "Display"
        Modes        "1152x864_75.00"
    EndSubSection
    Defaultdepth    24
EndSection

Section "ServerLayout"
    Identifier    "Default Layout"
  screen "Default Screen"
EndSection
Section "Module"
    Load        "glx"
EndSection

do not push your monitor beyond the specs its unsafe and costly.
